# Revenue Attribution Diagnostic — Acme SaaS

**Generated:** 2026-07-15T20:08:33.628Z
**Primary paid channel:** meta
**Markets:** EU, US
**Monthly attributed revenue (baseline):** $250,000

> 🇪🇺 EU market detected — consent + consent-mode are **non-optional** (GDPR).

## Headline

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Estimated monthly revenue leak | **$130,000** |
| Estimated annual revenue leak | **$1,560,000** |
| Audit fee | $5,000 |
| 10× guarantee | MET |

> ✅ **Guarantee met:** estimated annual leak ($1,560,000) ≥ 10× audit fee ($5,000). You pay.

## Tracking Default audit

| | Component | Status | Effective leak | Annual $ |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| ❌ | Meta Conversions API (CAPI) | missing | 12.0% | $360,000 |
| ❌ | Attribution model (data-driven, not last-click) | missing | 10.0% | $300,000 |
| ❌ | Lifecycle/revenue events (LTV, refund, churn) | missing | 9.0% | $270,000 |
| ❌ | MMM-lite (media mix modeling, lightweight) | missing | 7.0% | $210,000 |
| ❌ | Google Enhanced Conversions | missing | 6.0% | $180,000 |
| 🟡 | Server-side GTM (ssGTM) | partial | 4.0% | $120,000 |
| ❌ | Consent management (GDPR/TCPA) | missing | 4.0% | $120,000 |
| ✅ | GA4 (proper config) | present | 0.0% | — |

**Reading the table:** "present" = verified firing, 0 leak. "partial" = present but
incomplete, half the risk remains. "missing"/"broken" = full estimated leak applied.
Leak fractions are conservative, industry-report-derived and **[composite — to be
replaced with BAZ empirical data after the first 10 diagnostics]**.

## What this means

The single biggest hole is **Meta Conversions API (CAPI)**
($360,000/yr). Closing the top 3 typically recovers
**59.6%** of the estimated annual leak — without changing
ad spend, creative, or offer.

> BAZ doctrine: revenue not vanity. We instrument first, then grow. The 90-day plan
> below sequences the fixes by $ impact.
plan:
# 90-Day Plan — Acme SaaS

Sequenced by $ impact. Each fix has a named owner (BAZ) + an exit criterion
(measurable: the component status moves to "present" and the leak drops to 0).

## Days 0–30 — instrument the biggest holes
  - [ ] Fix **Meta Conversions API (CAPI)** — recovers ~$360,000/yr (12.0% effective leak)

**Exit criterion:** top-2 leaks closed; estimated monthly leak reduced by ≥50%.

## Days 31–60 — close the mid-tier gaps
  - [ ] Fix **Attribution model (data-driven, not last-click)** — recovers ~$300,000/yr (10.0% effective leak)
  - [ ] Fix **Lifecycle/revenue events (LTV, refund, churn)** — recovers ~$270,000/yr (9.0% effective leak)

**Exit criterion:** estimated annual leak ≤ $312,000 (80% reduction).

## Days 61–90 — institutionalize + hand off
  - [ ] Fix **MMM-lite (media mix modeling, lightweight)** — recovers ~$210,000/yr (7.0% effective leak)
  - [ ] Fix **Google Enhanced Conversions** — recovers ~$180,000/yr (6.0% effective leak)
  - [ ] Fix **Server-side GTM (ssGTM)** — recovers ~$120,000/yr (4.0% effective leak)
  - [ ] Fix **Consent management (GDPR/TCPA)** — recovers ~$120,000/yr (4.0% effective leak)

**Exit criterion:**
  - [ ] BAZ Tracking Default fully "present" on the live dashboard
  - [ ] Client team trained on reading the revenue-not-vanity report
  - [ ] Decision: convert to Core/Growth retainer or hand off

> Next logical yes (Deiss lifecycle): the diagnostic → retainer. BAZ's offer:
> Core ($6.5–9.5k/mo, one channel) or Growth ($18–28k/mo, full integrated stack).
